# People — Test Source

## Saints

### St. Augustine (Aurelius Augustinus)
- **Slug**: `saint/augustine`
- **Dates**: 354–430
- **Role**: Doctor of the Church, Bishop of Hippo
- **Occurrences**:
  - `#section-one-p1` — teaches that Sacred Scripture contains all wisdom
    text: "St. Augustine, in his great work On Christian Doctrine, teaches that Sacred Scripture contains all wisdom necessary for salvation."
  - `#section-one-p3` — converted from Manichaeism by reading Paul
    text: "The reading of Paul was able not only to join the heretic Augustine to the orthodox"

### St. Jerome (Hieronymus)
- **Slug**: `saint/jerome`
- **Dates**: c. 342–420
- **Occurrences**:
  - `#section-one-p2` — "Ignorance of the Scriptures is ignorance of Christ"
    text: "Ignorance of the Scriptures is ignorance of Christ."
  - `#section-two-p3` — Called "the phoenix of his age"
    text: "St. Jerome, the phoenix of his age, who devoted himself entirely to sacred Letters"
  - `#nonexistent-paragraph` — This reference has a bad paragraph ID
    text: "This text does not exist anywhere in the document."
